question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes CommonProperties to be re-initialized with different shard/realm values (e.g., via a config refresh), so that SystemEntity's lazy-initialized EntityId fields (addressBookFile101, addressBookFile102, etc.) return stale shard/realm combinations that no longer match the live network, causing incorrect entity resolution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy getter pattern on SystemEntity fields (e.g., addressBookFile101(), feeScheduleFile()) so that during a network partition that triggers a context refresh, the lazy fields are computed from a stale CommonProperties instance, causing system entity IDs to be permanently wrong for the lifetime of the bean?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the Spring context to restart, triggering CommonProperties.init() to call INSTANCE.set(this) with a new instance, while existing beans still hold a reference to the old CommonProperties instance, creating an inconsistent state where different beans use different shard/realm values?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delays Spring context initialization so that getInstance() is called before init() completes, throwing an IllegalStateException that propagates to the user as an unhandled 500 error, leaking internal class names and stack traces?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static INSTANCE AtomicReference in CommonProperties to observe that during a network partition causing a partial context reload, getInstance() may return a CommonProperties object whose DatabaseStartupProperties has been reset to defaults (enabled=true, timeout=5min), causing the application to block for 5 minutes on every restart attempt?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpelHelper bean registered for reflection via CommonRuntimeHints to invoke SpelHelper methods (isNullOrEmpty, hashCode) through a SpEL injection vulnerability in another component, using the network partition as a distraction to mask the exploitation attempt in logs?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hashCode(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpelHelper.hashCode(byte[]) method registered for reflection in CommonRuntimeHints to pass an extremely large byte array via a SpEL expression, causing excessive CPU consumption during a network partition when the application is already under stress?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isNullOrEmpty()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpelHelper.isNullOrEmpty(Collection<?>) method via a SpEL expression to pass a Collection implementation with a side-effecting isEmpty() method, causing unexpected behavior during cache evaluation when the application is recovering from a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: registerHints()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reflection registration of Caffeine cache classes (SIA, SSMSA, SSR, etc.) in CommonRuntimeHints to enumerate available cache implementations via reflection during a network partition, identifying cache keys that could be poisoned when connectivity is restored?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that jdbcUrl, username, and password are resolved from DataSourceProperties before being overridden by JdbcConnectionDetails, so that during a network partition that causes the JdbcConnectionDetails provider to fail, the application falls back to potentially less-secure DataSourceProperties credentials without any warning?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ObjectProvider<JdbcConnectionDetails> detailsProvider.getIfAvailable() call to observe that if the JdbcConnectionDetails bean becomes unavailable during a network partition (e.g., due to a Vault or secrets manager outage), the application silently falls back to plaintext DataSourceProperties credentials, potentially using weaker or expired credentials?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcProperties object in waitForDatabase() is created once and reused across all retry iterations, so that if the JDBC driver mutates the Properties object during a failed connection attempt, subsequent retries use corrupted connection properties, causing all retries to fail even after the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of exponential backoff in the retry loop (fixed Uninterruptibles.sleepUninterruptibly(properties.getInterval())) to cause a thundering herd when multiple mirror node instances simultaneously recover from a network partition, all retrying at the same fixed interval and overwhelming the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that DriverManager.getConnection() is a global, synchronized operation in some JVM implementations to cause a thread contention bottleneck during a network partition, where multiple DatabaseWaiter instances block each other and extend the effective outage duration beyond the configured timeout?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes DNS resolution for the jdbcUrl hostname to fail, so that DriverManager.getConnection() throws a different exception type than a connection refused error, and the catch (Exception e) block logs only the message without the full stack trace, hiding the DNS failure from operators?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the TCP connection to be accepted but then silently dropped (black-hole routing), so that DriverManager.getConnection() hangs until the OS TCP timeout (which may be minutes), far exceeding the configured connectTimeout JDBC property if the driver does not honor it?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the database to accept the TCP connection but never send the authentication challenge, so that the JDBC driver hangs in the authentication phase beyond the connectTimeout, because connectTimeout only covers TCP establishment and not the full handshake?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that new HikariDataSource(config) starts background connection maintenance threads that are not subject to the waitForDatabase() timeout, so that after a network partition, these threads continue attempting reconnection indefinitely, consuming CPU and network resources even if the application is supposed to have failed fast?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an explicit connectionTestQuery or healthCheckRegistry in the HikariConfig bean to ensure that during a network partition, HikariCP does not detect broken connections until they are actually used by a request, causing the first N requests after a partition to fail where N equals the pool size?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Lazy DataSource initialization to observe that if the network partition occurs between application startup and the first request, the DataSource is initialized against a partitioned network, and the waitForDatabase() call within dataSource() will block the first user request for up to the full timeout duration?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the IllegalStateException thrown at the end of waitForDatabase() includes the full timeout duration string in its message, leaking internal configuration values (e.g., 'Database did not become available within PT5M') that reveal the retry window an attacker has to maintain a partition?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that waitForDatabase() logs connection failure messages at INFO level (not WARN or ERROR) to ensure that during a sustained network partition, the log volume at INFO level is so high that it fills disk storage, causing a secondary denial-of-service through log exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the e.getMessage() logging in the catch block of waitForDatabase() to observe that if the JDBC driver includes the full jdbcUrl (including credentials embedded in the URL) in the exception message, the password is logged in plaintext during every retry of a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that occurs after databaseWaiter.waitForDatabase() returns but before hikariConfig.copyStateTo(config) completes, causing the HikariConfig state to be partially copied and resulting in a DataSource with inconsistent pool settings that causes intermittent failures?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CustomJsonFormatMapper being set as the JSON_FORMAT_MAPPER Hibernate property to observe that during a network partition causing a Hibernate session to be invalidated, the mapper's delegate JacksonJsonFormatMapper retains a reference to a closed session, causing NullPointerExceptions on subsequent requests?
